module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Colimits
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed

/-!
# The monoidal category structure on presheaves of modules

Given a presheaf of commutative rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`, we construct
the monoidal category structure on the category of presheaves of modules
`PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`. The tensor product `M₁ ⊗ M₂` is defined
as the presheaf of modules which sends `X : Cᵒᵖ` to `M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X`.

## Notes

This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop
"Formalizing algebraic geometry" in June 2024.

-/

@[expose] public section

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory BraidedCategory Category Limits

universe v u v₁ u₁

variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

instance (X : Cᵒᵖ) : CommRing ((R ⋙ forget₂ _ RingCat).obj X) :=
  inferInstanceAs (CommRing (R.obj X))

namespace PresheafOfModules

namespace Monoidal

variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Auxiliary definition for `tensorObj`. -/
noncomputable def tensorObjMap {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X ⟶
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y) :=
  ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorLift (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ M₂.map f m₂)
    (by
      intro m₁ m₁' m₂
      dsimp +instances
      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul])
    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₁.map_smul]; rfl)
    (by
      intro m₁ m₂ m₂'
      dsimp +instances
      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add])
    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₂.map_smul, TensorProduct.tmul_smul (r := R.map f a)]; rfl)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The tensor product of two presheaves of modules. -/
@[simps obj]
noncomputable def tensorObj : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
  obj X := M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X
  map f := tensorObjMap M₁ M₂ f
  map_id X := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
    intro m₁ m₂
    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
    simp
    rfl) -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_apply` doesn't get picked up due to type mismatch
  map_comp f g := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
    intro m₁ m₂
    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
    simp +instances)

variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma tensorObj_map_tmul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (m₁ : M₁.obj X) (m₂ : M₂.obj X) :
    DFunLike.coe (α := (M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X :))

content:
=== SheafOfModules structure body ===
/-- A sheaf of modules is a presheaf of modules such that the underlying presheaf
of abelian groups is a sheaf. -/
structure SheafOfModules where
  /-- the underlying presheaf of modules of a sheaf of modules -/
  val : PresheafOfModules.{v} R.obj
  isSheaf : Presheaf.IsSheaf J val.presheaf

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {R}

/-- A morphism between sheaves of modules is a morphism between the underlying
presheaves of modules. -/
@[ext]
structure Hom (X Y : SheafOfModules.{v} R) where
  /-- a morphism between the underlying presheaves of modules -/
  val : X.val ⟶ Y.val

instance : Category (SheafOfModules.{v} R) where
  Hom := Hom
  id _ := ⟨𝟙 _⟩
  comp f g := ⟨f.val ≫ g.val⟩

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext {X Y : SheafOfModules.{v} R} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h : f.val = g.val) : f = g :=
  Hom.ext h

@[simp]
lemma id_val (X : SheafOfModules.{v} R) : Hom.val (𝟙 X) = 𝟙 X.val := rfl

@[simp, reassoc]
=== unit def body ===
variable [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

variable (R) in
/-- The obvious free sheaf of modules of rank `1`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def unit : SheafOfModules R where
  val := PresheafOfModules.unit R.obj
  isSheaf := ((sheafCompose J (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj R).property

/-- The bijection `(unit R ⟶ M) ≃ M.sections` for `M : SheafOfModules R`. -/
noncomputable def unitHomEquiv (M : SheafOfModules R) :
    (unit R ⟶ M) ≃ M.sections :=
  (fullyFaithfulForget R).homEquiv.trans M.val.unitHomEquiv

@[simp]
lemma unitHomEquiv_apply_coe (M : SheafOfModules R) (f : unit R ⟶ M) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    (M.unitHomEquiv f).val X = f.val.app X (1 : R.obj.obj X) := rfl

lemma unitHomEquiv_comp_apply {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
    (f : unit R ⟶ M) (p : M ⟶ N) :
    N.unitHomEquiv (f ≫ p) = sectionsMap p (M.unitHomEquiv f) := rfl

content:
=== monoidal unit in Presheaf/Monoidal ===

end Monoidal

open Monoidal

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
    MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
  tensorObj := tensorObj
  whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
  whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
  tensorHom := tensorHom
  tensorUnit := unit _
  associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _)
    (fun _ _ _ ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext₃' (by intros; rfl))
  leftUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (λ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
    ext m
    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
    erw [leftUnitor_inv_apply, leftUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
    rfl))
  rightUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (ρ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
    ext m
    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
    erw [rightUnitor_inv_apply, rightUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
    rfl))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
    MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
  tensorHom_def _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_def
  id_tensorHom_id _ _ := by ext1; apply id_tensorHom_id
  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_comp_tensorHom
  whiskerLeft_id M₁ M₂ := by
    ext1 X
    apply MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_id (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
  id_whiskerRight _ _ := by
    ext1 X
    apply MonoidalCategory.id_whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
  associator_naturality _ _ _ := by ext1; apply associator_naturality
  leftUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply leftUnitor_naturality
  rightUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply rightUnitor_naturality
  pentagon _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply pentagon
  triangle _ _ := by ext1; apply triangle

open BraidedCategory

instance symmetricCategory :
    SymmetricCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
  braiding M₁ M₂ :=
    isoMk (fun X ↦ braiding (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X))
      (fun _ _ f ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl))
  braiding_naturality_right _ _ _ _ := by
    ext : 1
    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
  braiding_naturality_left _ _ := by
    ext : 1
    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
  hexagon_forward _ _ _ := by
    ext : 1
    apply hexagon_forward (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
  hexagon_reverse _ _ _ := by
    ext : 1
    apply hexagon_reverse (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
  symmetry _ _ := by
    ext : 1
    apply SymmetricCategory.symmetry (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))

section

variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))

lemma tensorObj_obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    (M₁ ⊗ M₂).obj X =
      MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) := rfl

attribute [local simp] tensorObj_obj

variable {M₂ M₃} in
@[simp]
lemma whiskerLeft_app (f : M₂ ⟶ M₃) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    dsimp% (M₁ ◁ f).app X = whiskerLeft (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (f.app X) :=
  rfl

variable {M₁ M₂} in
@[simp]
lemma whiskerRight_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (M₃ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    dsimp% (f ▷ M₃).app X = whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (M₃.obj X) := rfl

variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄} in
@[simp]
lemma tensorHom_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    dsimp% (f ⊗ₘ g).app X =
      MonoidalCategory.tensorHom (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (g.app X) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma leftUnitor_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).hom.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).hom :=
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma leftUnitor_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).inv.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).inv := by
  rfl

import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf

/-!
# Section graded ring infrastructure, Layer 1: tensor powers of a sheaf of modules

This file builds the Mathlib-absent infrastructure of
`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`, Layer 1
(`sec:sgr_tensor_powers`): the tensor product, tensor powers, and twists of
sheaves of modules over a scheme `X`, together with the canonical
tensor-power comparison isomorphism.

The category `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` of sheaves of modules
over a scheme carries **no** monoidal structure in Mathlib (the structure sheaf
varies the base ring over opens).  Mathlib *does* supply:

* the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of **presheaves** of modules
  `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal`), and
* the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`
  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification`).

We therefore build the tensor product of sheaves of modules as the sheafification
of the objectwise (presheaf) tensor product, following
[Stacks, Tag 01CA].

## Main definitions

* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification` — the scheme-level
  sheafification functor `X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) —
  `F ⊗ G := (F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)^#`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow` (`def:sheafTensorPow`) —
  the `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`) —
  the `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPowAdd` (`lem:sheafTensorPow_add`) —
  the canonical comparison isomorphism `L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}`.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The scheme-level sheafification functor, sending a presheaf of modules over a
scheme `X` to its associated sheaf of modules `X.Modules`.  It is the
`PresheafOfModules.sheafification` functor for the identity morphism of the
underlying presheaf of rings (which is locally bijective). -/
noncomputable def sheafification : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)

/-- The tensor product of two sheaves of modules over a scheme, defined as the
sheafification of the objectwise tensor product presheaf
(Mathlib `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  See [Stacks, Tag 01CA]
(`def:sheafTensorObj`). -/
noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
  sheafification.obj
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

presented in the exact form `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`

    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

input.old_string:
noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
  sheafification.obj

input.new_string:
noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
  sheafification.obj
    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))

/-- The structure sheaf as a sheaf of modules over itself: the unit object of the
tensor product, i.e. the zeroth tensor power `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X`
(`lem:moduleUnit_mathlib`). -/
noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf

/-- The `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules over a scheme, defined
by recursion: `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X` (the unit module) and
`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/
noncomputable def tensorPow (L : X.Modules) : ℕ → X.Modules
  | 0 => unitModule X
  | (m + 1) => tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L

@[simp] lemma tensorPow_zero (L : X.Modules) : tensorPow L 0 = unitModule X := rfl

@[simp] lemma tensorPow_succ (L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) :
    tensorPow L (m + 1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L := rfl

/-- The `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules `F` by the `m`-th
tensor power of a line bundle `L` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`).  This is the
degree-`m` carrier of the section graded module. -/
noncomputable def moduleTensorPow (F L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) : X.Modules :=
  tensorObj F (tensorPow L m)

@[simp] lemma moduleTensorPow_zero (F L : X.Modules) :
    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

@[simp] lemma moduleTensorPow_zero (F L : X.Modules) :
    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl

/-!
### The tensor-power comparison isomorphism

`tensorPowAdd` is the canonical isomorphism
`L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}` of [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`lem:sheafTensorPow_add`).

**Status (iter-047): documented typed `sorry`.** Proving it requires the
*monoidal* (associator + unitor) structure on the category `X.Modules` of
sheaves of modules — concretely the natural isomorphisms

* `tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G`  (left unitor), and
* `tensorObj (tensorObj A B) C ≅ tensorObj A (tensorObj B C)`  (associator),

descended through sheafification from the symmetric monoidal structure on
`X.PresheafOfModules` (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  Mathlib supplies
the monoidal structure only on **presheaves** of modules and, separately, on
`Sheaf J A` for a *fixed* monoidal `A` (`CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`); it has
**no** monoidal structure on `SheafOfModules R` over a sheaf of rings `R`.
Building it amounts to showing the module sheafification functor
`sheafification : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules` is (strong/lax) monoidal,
i.e. the comparison map `sheafification.obj (P ⊗ Q) ⟶ ...` assembling
`sheafification.obj P ⊗_sh sheafification.obj Q` is an isomorphism, which in turn
uses that the sheafification unit `η` is a "local isomorphism" stable under
tensoring (Day-convolution / reflective-monoidal-localization machinery,
`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`).

**Decomposition for the next iteration (handoff):**
1. `sheafificationCounitIso (G : X.Modules) : sheafification.obj ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ≅ G`
   — the counit of `sheafification ⊣ toPresheafOfModules`, an iso because the
   right adjoint (`SheafOfModules.forget`) is fully faithful.  GENUINELY
   AVAILABLE today (no missing infra).
2. `tensorObjUnitIso (G : X.Modules) : tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G`
   — presheaf left unitor `λ_` followed by (1).  Available once the identification
   `(toPresheafOfModules X).obj (unitModule X) = 𝟙_ (MonoidalPresheaf X)` is set up.
3. `sheafificationMonoidalComparison` — strong-monoidality of `sheafification`
   (`sheafification.obj P ⊗_sh sheafification.obj Q ≅ sheafification.obj (P ⊗ Q)`).
   THE MISSING INGREDIENT.  Route: instantiate
   `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal` for the reflective localization
   `sheafification ⊣ forget`, checking the localizer (local isomorphisms) is
   stable under `⊗` for presheaves of modules.
4. `tensorPowAdd` then follows by induction on `m` (base case = (2), step =
   associator from (3) + the recursive definition of `tensorPow`).
-/

/-- The canonical comparison isomorphism `L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}`
([Stacks, Tag 01CU], `lem:sheafTensorPow_add`).

The intended construction is by induction on `m`: the base case `m = 0` is the
left unitor `tensorObj (unitModule X) (tensorPow L m') ≅ tensorPow L m'`, and the
inductive step uses the associator of the sheaf tensor product together with the
recursive definition `tensorPow L (m+1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L`.  Both the
unitor and the associator descend through sheafification from
`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`; the latter requires the
strong-monoidality of the module sheafification functor, which is not yet in
Mathlib.  See the decomposition above. -/
noncomputable def tensorPowAdd (L : X.Modules) (m m' : ℕ) :
    tensorObj (tensorPow L m) (tensorPow L m') ≅ tensorPow L (m + m') :=
  sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

noncomputable def restrictScalars (α : R ⟶ R') :
    PresheafOfModules.{v} R' ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R where
  obj M' := M'.restrictScalarsObj α
  map φ' :=
    { app := fun X ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom).map (Hom.app φ' X)
      naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ by
        ext x
        exact naturality_apply φ' f x }

instance (α : R ⟶ R') : (restrictScalars.{v} α).Additive where

instance : (restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full

instance (α : R ⟶ R') : (restrictScalars α).Faithful where
  map_injective h := (toPresheaf R').map_injective ((toPresheaf R).congr_map h)

/-- The isomorphism `restrictScalars α ⋙ toPresheaf R ≅ toPresheaf R'` for any
morphism of presheaves of rings `α : R ⟶ R'`. -/

    restrictScalars.{v} α ⋙ toPresheaf R ≅ toPresheaf R' := Iso.refl _

## Key findings (in task_results)
- **Monoidal-instance wiring** (the warned-about block): `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` won't fire on `X.PresheafOfModules` (higher-order unification). Fixed via `abbrev MonoidalPresheaf X := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)` — defeq but in the instance-matchable form. (Trap: unqualified `PresheafOfModules` resolves to the *scheme* abbrev.)
- **Counit iso trap:** per-component `IsIso (adj.counit.app G)` won't synthesize; the whole-NatTrans `counit_isIso_of_R_fully_faithful` → `(asIso adj.counit).app G` works.

Next-iter route: build a stalkwise-iso⟹iso criterion for `X.Modules`, or port `CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal` to `SheafOfModules`. Base case + braiding + unitors are already built, so only the associator remains.

prompt:
You are a prover agent for project 'Quot-Foundations'. Current stage: prover.
Archon iteration: 047.
Project directory: /home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations
Project state directory: /home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/.archon
Read /home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/.archon/AGENTS.md for your role, then read /home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/.archon/PROGRESS.md.
Check your .lean file for /- USER: ... -/ comments for file-specific hints.

IMPORTANT:
- You own ONLY the file assigned below. Do NOT edit any other .lean file.
- Write your results to /home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md when done.
- Do NOT edit PROGRESS.md, task_pending.md, or task_done.md.
- Missing Mathlib infrastructure is NEVER a valid reason to leave a sorry.
- NEVER revert to a bare sorry. Always leave your partial proof attempt in the code.

Blueprint chapter for your file: blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex
- Read it BEFORE writing any Lean code — it contains the informal proof
  written by the plan agent.
- After you formalize a declaration, mark its blueprint environment with \leanok.
- If the chapter file does not yet exist, create it with a minimal \chapter block
  and note in your task_results that the plan agent should flesh it out.

## Blueprint dependency graph (leandag)

You can navigate the project's dependency graph read-only to find the
proven lemmas/defs your goal can lean on (with the exact `\lean{}`
names to apply) instead of re-deriving them. `archon` is on PATH;
`--json` prints parseable JSON to stdout (banner to stderr):

```
archon dag-query ancestors --node <blueprint-label-of-your-target>   # proven deps available to you
archon dag-query node      --node <label>                            # one declaration's status
```

It is a navigation aid only — the proof obligation and your blueprint
chapter remain the source of truth.

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. List each new non-private declaration (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) so the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence the dag agent built is restored next iter. Closing 0 project sorries while adding 3 unreported helpers does not advance the project — it quietly re-introduces the isolation the DAG phase worked to eliminate. Reporting them is mandatory.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

content: I'll start by orienting myself with the project state and my assigned file.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

9	/-!
10	# Generic flatness of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
11	
12	This file packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4 **generic flatness**
13	theorem: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent `𝓞_X`-module on a
14	finite-type morphism `X ⟶ S` becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open
15	subscheme of `S`. This is the inductive engine underlying the
16	flattening-stratification construction of Grothendieck's existence theorem for
17	the Quot scheme.
18	
19	Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the *intended* substantive type
20	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
21	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a `sorry`
22	body where the proof is not yet supplied. The substantive generic-flatness
23	proof is deep (Nitsure~\S4: prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
24	clearing denominators).
25	
26	The blueprint-pinned declaration is:
27	
28	- `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem) — **generic flatness**
29	  [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf
30	  on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. Its
31	  algebraic form (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin) is the
32	  statement that a finite module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian
33	  domain becomes free after inverting one non-zero element of the base.
34	
35	The file-internal `GenericFreeness` namespace lands the **finite-module /
36	finite-morphism special case** of the algebraic form axiom-clean, as a reusable
37	building block for the dévissage's leaves.
38	
39	## Note on type expressivity
40	
41	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
42	`genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V` and
43	substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers are necessary
44	(without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
45	
46	## Mathlib status
47	
48	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`
49	(morphism-level), `IsImmersion`, `IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`,
50	`LocallyOfFiniteType`, and the dévissage / Noether-normalisation infrastructure
51	the algebraic generic-flatness argument consumes. It does NOT yet provide the
52	polynomial-ring core of generic freeness, nor the geometric `genericFlatness`
53	statement packaging it over a noetherian integral base.
54	
55	## References
56	
57	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`.
58	Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained
59	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tag 00HB (module flat).
60	-/

64	universe u
65	
66	open CategoryTheory Limits
67	
68	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
69	
70	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — algebraic generic freeness (finite case)
71	
72	This section builds the **module-theoretic** generic-freeness statements that
73	underlie the geometric `genericFlatness` (blueprint
74	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness").
75	
76	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
77	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
78	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
79	clearing denominators). Mathlib already supplies most of the dévissage
80	machinery (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`,
81	`ModuleCat.free_shortExact`, `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`), but it does
82	**not** yet contain the polynomial-ring core (generic freeness for a finite
83	module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]`). See the file `task_results` handoff for the
84	precise decomposition of the remaining gap.
85	
86	What we *can* land axiom-clean here is the **finite-module / finite-morphism
87	case**: when `M` is finite as an `A`-module (in particular when `B` is
88	module-finite over `A`), generic freeness follows directly from
89	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` applied at the
90	generic point `Frac A`, where `M ⊗_A Frac A` is a finite vector space hence
91	free. This is a genuine special case of the algebraic generic-freeness
92	theorem (the case of a *finite* morphism `X → S`), and a reusable building
93	block for the dévissage's leaves. -/
94	
95	namespace GenericFreeness
96	
97	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
98	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
99	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.
100	
101	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
102	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
103	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
104	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
105	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
106	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
107	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
108	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
109	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
110	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
111	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
112	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
113	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
114	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
115	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩
116	
117	/-- **Generic flatness, finite-module case.** The flatness form of
118	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
119	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
120	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
121	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
122	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
123	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
124	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
125	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
126	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
127	  haveI := hfree
128	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩
129	
130	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
131	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
132	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
133	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
134	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
135	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
136	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
137	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
138	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
139	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
140	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
141	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
142	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
143	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
144	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
145	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
146	
147	/-! ### Dévissage chain for the finite-type residue (Nitsure §4)
148	
149	The following sub-lemmas decompose the surviving residue of
150	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` — the case where `M` is finite over the finite-type
151	`A`-algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A` — into the classical Nitsure §4
152	induction. They are ordered so each step uses only earlier blocks (or Mathlib
153	anchors). Throughout, `A` is a noetherian domain with fraction field
154	`K = Frac A`, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module with
155	the compatible `A`-module structure via the scalar tower `A → B → M`. -/
156	
157	/-- **L1 — torsion base case `M_K = 0`** (`lem:gf_torsion_base`, Nitsure §4 base
158	case of the induction). If the generic localisation `M_K = K ⊗_A M` (encoded as
159	`LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M`) is trivial and `M` is a finite
160	`B`-module, then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `f • M = 0`; consequently
161	`M_f = 0` is free over `A_f`.
162	
163	The hypothesis `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)` is the
164	formal encoding of `M_K = 0`: localising `M` at the non-zero divisors of the
165	domain `A` inverts exactly the non-zero elements, so `M_K = S^{-1}M` with
166	`S = A \ {0}`. Each generator of the finite `B`-module `M` is then killed by a
167	non-zero element of `A`; the product `f` of those annihilators (non-zero since
168	`A` is a domain) annihilates all of `M`, whence `M_f` is subsingleton and free. -/
169	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion
170	    (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
171	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
172	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
173	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
174	    (htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)) :
175	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
176	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
177	  -- The `A`/`B` scalar actions commute (A acts through `algebraMap A B`).
178	  have hcomm : ∀ (a : A) (b : B) (x : M), a • b • x = b • a • x := fun a b x => by
179	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a (b • x), ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a x,
180	      smul_smul, smul_smul, mul_comm]
181	  -- Torsion: every element of `M` is killed by a non-zero divisor of `A`.
182	  rw [LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff] at htors
183	  choose r hr using htors
184	  -- `r m ∈ A⁰` and `(r m) • m = 0`.
185	  -- A finite `B`-generating set of `M`.
186	  obtain ⟨S, hS⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top : (⊤ : Submodule B M).FG)
187	  -- The product of the chosen annihilators of the generators.
188	  set f : A := ∏ x ∈ S, r x with hf_def
189	  have hfmem : f ∈ nonZeroDivisors A := prod_mem fun x _ => (hr x).1
190	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hfmem
191	  -- `f` annihilates every generator, hence all of `span B S = ⊤ = M`.
192	  have hgen : ∀ x ∈ S, f • x = (0 : M) := by
193	    intro x hx
194	    classical
195	    rw [hf_def, ← Finset.prod_erase_mul S r hx, mul_smul, (hr x).2, smul_zero]
196	  have hfM : ∀ m : M, f • m = (0 : M) := by
197	    intro m
198	    have hmem : m ∈ Submodule.span B (↑S : Set M) := by rw [hS]; exact Submodule.mem_top
199	    induction hmem using Submodule.span_induction with
200	    | mem x hx => exact hgen x hx
201	    | zero => exact smul_zero f
202	    | add x y _ _ ihx ihy => rw [smul_add, ihx, ihy, add_zero]
203	    | smul b x _ ih => rw [hcomm, ih, smul_zero]
204	  -- `f • M = 0` ⟹ `M_f` subsingleton ⟹ free over `A_f`.
205	  refine ⟨f, hf0, ?_⟩
206	  haveI : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
207	    LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m => ⟨f, Submonoid.mem_powers f, hfM m⟩
208	  exact Module.Free.of_subsingleton _ _
209	
210	/-- **L3a — localisation of a short exact sequence is exact**
211	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact`, Nitsure §4). Given a short exact
212	sequence `0 → M' → M → M'' → 0` of `B`-modules and `f ∈ A`, restricting scalars
213	along `A → B` and localising every term at the powers of `f` yields a short
214	exact sequence `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules; the localised
215	`i_f` is injective and the localised `q_f` is surjective.
216	
217	Localisation of modules at a multiplicative set is exact, so it preserves the
218	injection `i`, the surjection `q`, and exactness at the middle term
219	(`LocalizedModule.map_injective` / `…map_surjective` / `…map_exact` applied to
220	the scalar-restricted maps). -/
221	theorem exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact
222	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A]
223	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
224	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
225	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
226	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
227	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
228	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
229	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
230	    (f : A) :
231	    Function.Injective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)) ∧
232	    Function.Surjective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) ∧
233	    Function.Exact (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A))
234	      (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) := by
235	  refine ⟨LocalizedModule.map_injective _ (i.restrictScalars A) hi,
236	    LocalizedModule.map_surjective _ (q.restrictScalars A) hq, ?_⟩
237	  exact LocalizedModule.map_exact (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)
238	    (q.restrictScalars A) hexact
239	
240	/-- **L3b — free transport across a finer localisation**
241	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport`, Nitsure §4). Let `N` be an
242	`A`-module and `f', f'' ∈ A` with `f = f' f''`. If `N_{f'}` is free over
243	`A_{f'}`, then `N_f` is free over `A_f`.
244	
245	Because `f = f' f''`, the localisation `A_f` is a localisation of `A_{f'}` at the
246	image of `f''` (`IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft` / `IsLocalization.Away.mul'`),
247	and `N_f` is the corresponding localisation of `N_{f'}`; a localisation of a free
248	module is free over the localised base ring (`Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`). -/
249	theorem free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul
250	    (A N : Type*) [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N]
251	    {f f' f'' : A} (hf : f = f' * f'')
252	    (hN' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f')
253	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N)) :
254	    Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
255	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
256	  subst hf
257	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away f')
258	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N) := hN'
259	  -- `A_f` also localises `f'' * f'` (= `f' * f''`).
260	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.Away (f'' * f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) := by
261	    rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance
262	  -- The ring map `A_{f'} → A_{f'f''}` and the `A`-scalar-tower it sits in.
263	  letI algA' : Algebra (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) :=
264	    (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft (R := A) (S := Localization.Away f')
265	      (P := Localization.Away (f' * f'')) f' f'').toAlgebra
266	  haveI htower : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) :=
267	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun a =>
268	      (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft_eq (S := Localization.Away f') f' f'' a).symm
269	  -- Restrict scalars along `A_{f'} → A_{f'f''}` to make `N_f` an `A_{f'}`-module.
270	  letI modA' : Module (Localization.Away f') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) :=
271	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')))
272	  haveI towerA'Af : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
273	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) :=
274	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
275	  haveI towerAA' : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f')
276	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) := by
277	    refine ⟨fun a a' x => ?_⟩
278	    have hcompA' : ∀ (b : Localization.Away f')
279	        (y : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N),
280	        b • y = algebraMap (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) b • y :=
281	      fun _ _ => rfl
282	    rw [hcompA' (a • a') x, hcompA' a' x, Algebra.smul_def, map_mul,
283	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) a,
284	      mul_smul, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
285	  -- `f'` acts invertibly on `N_f`: `f' * f''` does, and the commuting factor `f'` inherits it.
286	  have hunit : ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f',
287	      IsUnit (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N))
288	        (x : A)) := by
289	    have hprod : IsUnit (algebraMap A
290	        (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) (f' * f'')) :=
291	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units
292	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)
293	        ⟨f' * f'', Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
294	    rw [map_mul] at hprod
295	    have hcomm : Commute
296	        (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) f')
297	        (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) f'') :=
298	      (Commute.all _ _).map _
299	    have hf'unit := (hcomm.isUnit_mul_iff.mp hprod).1
300	    rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
301	    simpa [map_pow] using hf'unit.pow n
302	  -- The `A`-linear localisation map `N_{f'} → N_f`, upgraded to `A_{f'}`-linear.
303	  let φ : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N →ₗ[A]
304	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N :=
305	    IsLocalizedModule.lift (Submonoid.powers f') (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ N)
306	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ N) hunit
307	  let h : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N →ₗ[Localization.Away f']
308	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N :=
309	    LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers f') (Localization.Away f') φ
310	  -- `N_{f'}` is the base change of `N` to `A_{f'}`; the composite to `N_f` is the
311	  -- base change of `N` to `A_f`, so by cancellation `h` is the base change to `A_f`.
312	  have hbcA' : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away f')
313	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N) :=
314	    IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f') (Localization.Away f') _
315	  have hcomp : (LinearMap.restrictScalars A h) ∘ₗ
316	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N)
317	      = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N := by
318	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_extendScalarsOfIsLocalization]
319	    exact IsLocalizedModule.lift_comp _ _ _ hunit
320	  have hbcAf : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
321	      ((LinearMap.restrictScalars A h) ∘ₗ
322	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N)) := by
323	    rw [hcomp]
324	    exact IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (f' * f''))
325	      (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) _
326	  exact (hbcA'.of_comp hbcAf).free
327	
328	/-- **L3c — a short exact sequence with free ends has a free middle**
329	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split`, Nitsure §4). Let
330	`0 → P → Q → T → 0` be a short exact sequence of `R`-modules with both ends `P`
331	and `T` free over `R`. Then `Q` is free over `R`.
332	
333	The free quotient `T` is projective, so the surjection `Q → T` admits a section
334	(`Module.projective_lifting_property`); the splitting
335	(`Function.Exact.splitSurjectiveEquiv`) gives `Q ≃ₗ[R] P × T`, and `P × T` is
336	free, hence so is `Q` (`Module.Free.of_equiv`). -/
337	theorem free_of_shortExact_of_free_free
338	    (R P Q T : Type*) [CommRing R]
339	    [AddCommGroup P] [Module R P] [Module.Free R P]
340	    [AddCommGroup Q] [Module R Q]
341	    [AddCommGroup T] [Module R T] [Module.Free R T]
342	    (iota : P →ₗ[R] Q) (pi : Q →ₗ[R] T)
343	    (hiota : Function.Injective iota) (hpi : Function.Surjective pi)
344	    (hexact : Function.Exact iota pi) :
345	    Module.Free R Q := by
346	  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property pi LinearMap.id hpi
347	  obtain ⟨e, -⟩ := hexact.splitSurjectiveEquiv hiota ⟨l, hl⟩
348	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv e.symm
349	
350	/-- **L3 — splicing fact for a short exact sequence** (`lem:gf_splice_shortExact`,
351	Nitsure §4). Given a short exact sequence `0 → M' → M → M'' → 0` of `B`-modules
352	and non-zero `f', f'' ∈ A` such that `M'_{f'}` is free over `A_{f'}` and
353	`M''_{f''}` is free over `A_{f''}`, then for `f := f' f''` the localisation
354	`M_f` is free over `A_f`.
355	
356	Proof: localisation at the powers of `f` is exact, so it carries the SES to a
357	SES `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules. Both ends become free over
358	`A_f` (a free module over `A_{f'}` stays free under the further localisation
359	`A_{f'} → A_f`, via `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`), and a SES with free
360	(hence projective) quotient `M''_f` splits, so `M_f ≅ M'_f ⊕ M''_f` is free. -/
361	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact
362	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
363	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
364	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
365	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
366	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
367	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
368	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
369	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
370	    {f' f'' : A} (hf' : f' ≠ 0) (hf'' : f'' ≠ 0)
371	    (hM' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') M'))
372	    (hM'' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f'') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f'') M'')) :
373	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
374	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
375	  -- Take `f := f' * f''`, non-zero in the domain `A`.
376	  refine ⟨f' * f'', mul_ne_zero hf' hf'', ?_⟩
377	  -- (L3b) Both ends become free over `A_{f'f''}`.
378	  haveI hM'f : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
379	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M') :=
380	    free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul A M' (f := f' * f'') (f' := f') (f'' := f'') rfl hM'
381	  haveI hM''f : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
382	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M'') :=
383	    free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul A M'' (f := f' * f'') (f' := f'') (f'' := f')
384	      (mul_comm f' f'') hM''
385	  -- (L3a) The scalar-restricted SES stays exact after localising at the powers of `f'f''`.
386	  obtain ⟨hinj, hsurj, hex⟩ :=
387	    exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact A B M' M M'' i q hi hq hexact (f' * f'')
388	  -- (L3c) A short exact sequence of `A_{f'f''}`-modules with free ends has a free middle.
389	  exact free_of_shortExact_of_free_free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
390	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M')
391	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M)
392	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M'')
393	    (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) (i.restrictScalars A))
394	    (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) (q.restrictScalars A))
395	    hinj hsurj hex
396	
397	/-- **L4a — clearing one polynomial's denominators** (`lem:gf_clear_one_denominator`,
398	Nitsure §4 common-denominator step). For `A` a noetherian domain with fraction
399	field `K = Frac A` and a polynomial `p ∈ K[X_1,…,X_n]`, there is a non-zero
400	`g ∈ A` such that `p` lies in the image of the coefficient-extension map
401	`A_g[X_1,…,X_n] → K[X_1,…,X_n]`, where `A_g = Localization.Away g`; equivalently
402	every coefficient of `p` already lies in the subring `A_g ⊆ K`.
403	
404	The denominator-clearing engine: take `g` to be a common denominator of the
405	finitely many coefficients of `p` (Mathlib's
406	`IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples` over the support), so each coefficient
407	`c = a / g ∈ A_g`. The witness polynomial is the numerator polynomial scaled by
408	the unit `g⁻¹ ∈ A_g`. This is the shared primitive the Noether-normalisation L4
409	denominator-clear and the torsion-reindex (L5b) both consume. -/
410	theorem gf_clear_one_denominator
411	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
412	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (FractionRing A)) :
413	    ∃ (g : A) (hg : g ≠ 0),
414	      p ∈ Set.range (MvPolynomial.map
415	        (IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A)
416	          (show Submonoid.powers g ≤ Submonoid.comap (RingHom.id A) (nonZeroDivisors A) by
417	            rw [Submonoid.powers_le]
418	            simpa using mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg)
419	          : Localization.Away g →+* FractionRing A)) := by
420	  classical
421	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples (nonZeroDivisors A)
422	    p.support (fun i => MvPolynomial.coeff i p)
423	  refine ⟨s, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero s.2, ?_⟩
424	  have hle : Submonoid.powers (↑s : A) ≤ Submonoid.comap (RingHom.id A) (nonZeroDivisors A) := by
425	    rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact s.2
426	  set φ : Localization.Away (↑s : A) →+* FractionRing A :=
427	    IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A) hle with hφ
428	  have hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s : A)) ↑s) :=
429	    IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away (↑s : A))
430	      (⟨↑s, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩ : Submonoid.powers (↑s : A))
431	  have hnum : ∀ i, ∃ a : A, (algebraMap A (FractionRing A)) a =
432	      (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s) * MvPolynomial.coeff i p := by
433	    intro i
434	    by_cases hi : i ∈ p.support
435	    · obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := hs i hi
436	      exact ⟨a, by rw [ha, Algebra.smul_def]⟩
437	    · exact ⟨0, by rw [MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hi]; simp⟩
438	  choose a ha using hnum
439	  have hφs : φ (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)) ↑s)
440	      = algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s := by rw [hφ, IsLocalization.map_eq]; rfl
441	  have hee : (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
442	      * algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)) ↑s = 1 := by
443	    have h := Units.inv_mul hunit.unit
444	    rwa [hunit.unit_spec] at h
445	  have hφinv : φ (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
446	      * algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s = 1 := by
447	    rw [← hφs, ← map_mul, hee, map_one]
448	  -- The preimage polynomial: the numerator polynomial scaled by the unit `g⁻¹`.
449	  refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
450	        * (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)))
451	            (∑ i ∈ p.support, MvPolynomial.monomial i (a i))), ?_⟩
452	  apply MvPolynomial.ext
453	  intro i
454	  rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_map, MvPolynomial.coeff_C_mul, map_mul, MvPolynomial.coeff_map,
455	    IsLocalization.map_eq]
456	  have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff i (∑ j ∈ p.support, MvPolynomial.monomial j (a j)) = a i := by
457	    rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_sum]
458	    simp only [MvPolynomial.coeff_monomial, Finset.sum_ite_eq']
459	    by_cases hi : i ∈ p.support
460	    · rw [if_pos hi]
461	    · rw [if_neg hi]
462	      have hz : MvPolynomial.coeff i p = 0 := MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hi
463	      have h2 := ha i
464	      rw [hz, mul_zero] at h2
465	      exact (IsFractionRing.injective A (FractionRing A) (by rw [h2, map_zero])).symm
466	  rw [hcoeff, RingHom.id_apply, ha i, ← mul_assoc, hφinv, one_mul]
467	
468	/-- **L4 helper — a localisation lift into another injective localisation is injective.**
469	If `S` is a localisation of `R` at `M`, `g : R →+* P` sends `M` to units, and both
470	`algebraMap R S` and `g` are injective, then the induced `IsLocalization.lift hg : S →+* P`
471	is injective. (Used for the comparison maps `ν : B_g → B_K` and `ψ : A_g → K` in L4, both
472	localisations of a domain into a larger localisation.) -/
473	theorem isLocalization_lift_injective {R P : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing P]
474	    {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] {M : Submonoid R} [IsLocalization M S]
475	    {g : R →+* P} (hg : ∀ y : M, IsUnit (g (y : R)))
476	    (hSinj : Function.Injective (algebraMap R S))
477	    (hginj : Function.Injective g) :
478	    Function.Injective (IsLocalization.lift hg : S →+* P) := by
479	  rw [IsLocalization.lift_injective_iff]
480	  intro x y
481	  rw [hSinj.eq_iff, hginj.eq_iff]
482	
483	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
484	-- Deep nested localization instance synthesis (cf. `gf_torsion_reindex`) needs raised budgets.
485	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
486	-- The comparison-map (`ν`, `ψ`) + `aeval` assembly over stacked `Away` localizations is heavy.
487	/-- **L4 — Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators**
488	(`lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain
489	with fraction field `K`, and `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra that is a domain with
490	`B_K = K ⊗_A B ≠ 0`, there exist `g ∈ A`, `g ≠ 0`, a number `n`, and an
491	injective `A_g`-algebra map `φ : A_g[X_1,…,X_n] → B_g` (the `b_j := φ(X_j)`
492	algebraically independent over `K`) such that `B_g` is module-finite over
493	`A_g[X_1,…,X_n]` through `φ`.
494	
495	Here `A_g := Localization.Away g`, `B_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)`,
496	and module-finiteness is taken with respect to the `A_g[X]`-module structure on
497	`B_g` induced by `φ`.

1	\chapter{Flattening Stratification of a Coherent Sheaf}
2	\label{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4
6	% \emph{generic flatness} theorem: over a noetherian integral base, a coherent
7	% sheaf on a finite-type \(X \to S\) becomes flat over a non-empty open
8	% subscheme of \(S\). Both its algebraic form (generic freeness of a finite
9	% module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian domain) and its
10	% geometric form are recorded here as the prover-ready specification for
11	% \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}.
12	%
13	% This chapter is independently parallel-startable: it does not gate on the
14	% Route~A.1.a relative-Spec chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}.
15	
16	\section{Setup and motivation}
17	\label{sec:flatstrat_setup}
18	
19	Generic flatness is the foundational input to the flattening-stratification
20	construction underlying Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Quot scheme
21	(\cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}, \S4--\S5).  Its content is that flatness of a
22	coherent sheaf is a generic property over an integral base: over a noetherian
23	integral scheme \(S\), a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type morphism
24	\(X \to S\) becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open subscheme of
25	\(S\). Algebraically this is the statement that a finite module over a
26	finite-type algebra over a noetherian domain becomes free after inverting a
27	single non-zero element of the base ring.
28	
29	We work throughout with a noetherian (integral, where required) base scheme
30	\(S\), a finite-type morphism \(p : X \to S\), and a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
31	\(\F\).
32	
33	\section{Generic flatness}
34	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
35	
36	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
37	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
38	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
39	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
40	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
41	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
42	non-zero element of the base ring.
43	
44	\begin{theorem}
45	
46	
47	
48	\leanok
49	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
50	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}
52	  % NOTE (iter-022): CLOSED, axiom-clean {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}.
53	  % The Lean decl is single-universe `(A B M : Type u)` (was `Type*`): L5 and

56	  % geometric use (scheme patches are single-universe).

58	        lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators, lem:gf_polynomial_core, lem:gf_finite_module}

60	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
61	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
62	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
63	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
64	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
65	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
66	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
67	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
68	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
69	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
70	
71	\end{theorem}
72

81
